businessBackground: UK-listed B2B services group operating in **secure and sustainable business services for data, information, communications and assets** [FACT — FY25 RNS, p.1]. Three continuing-operations segments after December 2025 disposal of Harrow Green:
catalysts: 30 June 2026: FY2025 statutory accounts due at Companies House (refined 2026-04-27 from prior "Jun-Sep 2026" range)
October 2026: Two-tier workforce / Employment Rights Act effective
Ongoing 2026: £20m buyback execution (T1 Investec → T2 Canaccord; cumulative ~£1.38m / £18.6m of £20m remaining throu...

V1.name: Fundamentals Trajectory
V1.rationale: Multi-vector trajectory filing-evidenced: IM margin 27.3% → 28.1% (FY24 → FY25), Datashred 8.6% → 10.3% → 12.3% → 15%+E, Tech -£1.4m → £2.8m → double-digit-E, Synertec accretion + £5m+ digital savings (vs £3m plan). FY26 PBT guided ahead of £46.6m consensus. **Management alignment (Step 1 — structural):** filing-disclosed 1.5-2.0x leverage covenant + capital allocation framework (invest > dividend > leverage > buyback) + DC-only pension. **Step 2 (signals):** CEO Skinner +11,001 sh @ 272.69p Dec 2025; CFO Baker +25k @ 271.43p Aug 2024 + £5k Q2 2025 (pattern); £20m buyback running, 170k cancelled @ 242.71–245.75p Apr 13–17 2026 (primary-source corrected 2026-04-27); Harrow Green disposal (anti-empire-building). EXTREME held back: FY25 organic growth broadly flat (mostly acquisitions); statutory PBT £7.7m vs adj £40.6m structurally noisy via Synertec earn-out.

V2.name: Valuation Mismatch
V2.rationale: Claude PW-FV 406p / council PW-FV 404.9p / spot 254p = **+59-60% mismatch** (independent derivations match within 0.3%). Multi-method stress test: P/E 381p (+50%), SOTP 393p (+55%), thesis-independent generalist 392.4p (+54%) — every reasonable upside-anchored method 50-60% above spot. Implied scenario: market prices ~83-86% Bear-case weight despite filing-disclosed FY25 delivery + ahead-of-consensus FY26 guide. Market-vs-Me Q2 (2026-04-25): ~50% priced (box stability + partial FY26 guide), ~50% unpriced (Datashred 15%+, Tech double-digit, Synertec ex-postage, Hidden RE, multiple recovery). EXTREME held back: Bear FV 210p / 221.6p sit BELOW spot; no method shows mismatch >100% nor bear-case ≥ current price.

V3.name: Identifiable Mispricing Reason
V3.rationale: Bear case ARTICULABLE (AIM-listed mature roll-up + statutory-vs-adjusted noise + wasting-asset narrative + UK B2B consolidator track record). Drawdown Diagnosis: 5 enumerated causes — 4/5 TRANSIENT and addressed/in-motion (IHT-flow → buyback; statutory-noise → Synertec earn-out compression 4-yr window; wasting-asset → 22m box stability disclosed; sell-side modelling lag → FY26 management guidance). 1/5 STRUCTURAL (AIM-listing-derate-persistence) UNADDRESSED — softer reading: venue/liquidity discount not fundamental impairment, so V3 not capped at WEAK. Council MIA Lens 2: V3 ALREADY_PRICED at 5x EV/EBITDA. DEBATES "Consensus Pricing" OPEN 2026-04-22 pending H1 2026 interim re-test. STRONG ruled out: AIM-derate persistence genuinely unresolved.

V4.name: Downside Protection
V4.rationale: Pre-mortem: 9 failure modes enumerated. Highest-probability bounded (#1 IM organic decline 25-30% / -25-35% damage; #2 IM margin compression 15-20% / -15-20%; #8 IHT forced selling 20-25% / -15-25%). Tail risk #9 compound-failure ~14% probability / -35-50% damage (council-quantified, kill case). **Hard asset floor: NONE** — intangibles £310.0m > equity £219.7m → negative tangible equity ~£(90.3)m; freehold £37.9m + Hidden RE +£37m at upper bound = small vs £344m mcap. **Cash-flow floor: REAL** — FCF £42.9m / 12.5% yield at current price; even at -20% revenue stress ≥£25m / 7.3% yield. Lender behavior (Oct 2025 RCF refinance with 5-bank syndicate) inconsistent with distress. **DATA GAP: covenant trigger ratio + headroom % undisclosed** — binding constraint, council MIA explicitly cited as keeping V4 AVERAGE not STRONG. STRONG requires hard floor. EXTREME structurally out of reach (net debt £123.8m, not net cash).

V5.name: Thesis Simplicity
V5.rationale: Required conditions enumeration: 3 core (IM volume + IM margin + covenant headroom) all HOLDING + observable; 2 conditional value-add (Datashred 15%+, Tech double-digit) HOLDING-in-motion; 1 unconfirmed (multiple re-rate, structural). Council bear's null hypothesis lists 5 bull-required conditions (last one — AIM discount narrows — structural). Council bull thesis enumerated 4 conditions per council conviction agent. Q4 (market-vs-me 2026-04-25) specific binary path requires 3 simultaneous events at joint ~21%; broader path ~70%. STRONG requires 2 conditions both observable and HOLDING — collapsing IM-economics to single condition is loose; covenant headroom is a binary continuous-monitoring trigger. WEAK ruled out: all enumerated conditions HOLDING + observable, multiple supporting data points.

V6.name: Upside Optionality
V6.rationale: 7-8 conservatively-counted independent paths (independence-tested): #1 multiple re-rate 5x → 7-8x peer median (+35-45%), #2 Datashred margin doubling (+5-7%), #3 Tech double-digit (+3-5%), #4 Synertec ex-postage convergence + NHS Notify (+5-8%), #5 Hidden RE £55-75m vs £37.9m NBV (+10-15%, web-inference), #6 buyback compounding 6%/yr (mechanical), #7 M&A take-private (+30-50% premium, no public signal), #9 AI/digitisation revenue (aspirational). 3 paths show STRONG early evidence (Datashred, Tech, Buyback); 2 show MEDIUM (Synertec, property consolidation). Bull case 565p / +122% (incl Hidden RE) demonstrates torque. Council bull-optionality: 6 non-trivial vectors. EXTREME held back: operational paths (#2/#3/#4) correlated via public-sector demand-driver concentration ("public sector is both moat and correlation risk" per council MIA Lens 4); Hidden RE web-inference rather than filing-confirmed; M&A and AI aspirational.

V7.name: Evidence Quality
V7.rationale: Core thesis rests almost entirely on [FACT]-labeled audited filing data + verbatim management quotes: FY25 Annual Report 164pp + FY25 RNS prelim 42pp (bulk-filing-review 2026-04-22, within 12mo); 5 transcripts inline-cited; 22m box stability VERBATIM (Full-Year-Results-2025.md p.12); 20.8% adj op margin / 28.1% IM margin / FCF £42.9m / 1.9x leverage all OVERVIEW-table-cited; insider buys + buyback execution RNS-verified; council fair-value PW-FV 404.9p derived from filing-disclosed segment economics; Claude independent PW-FV 406p matches within 0.3%. **Critical DATA GAPs:** (1) RCF covenant trigger ratio + headroom % — bounded for V4 specifically; (2) H1 2026 forward items (organic IM, DWP run-rate, Synertec NHS conversion) — these are validation tests, not core-thesis assumptions; (3) Hidden RE precise £55-75m range is web-inference. EXTREME held back by covenant DATA GAP + Hidden-RE inference quality. No V1/V2/V4 caps fire.

V8.name: Catalyst Clarity
V8.rationale: Multiple specific dated catalysts: H1 2026 trading update ~Jul-Aug, **H1 2026 interim ~mid-Sep 2026 (PRIMARY)**, FY26 results ~Mar 2027, FY25 statutory accounts ~Jun-Sep 2026, £20m buyback over 12mo. Operational catalysts EVIDENTIAL (preconditions in motion: Datashred trajectory 8.6 → 12.3 → 15%E, Tech turnaround printed FY25, NHS Notify framework won Dec 2025, buyback executing). Buyback + dividend ARITHMETIC (~9% pa combined). **CONDITIONAL cap (per Q4 self-correction CONDITIONAL classification, joint specific path ~21%) applies** — additional mechanical catalysts (buyback) lift V8 above WEAK but dominant repricing magnitude (multiple re-rate +35-45%) is CONDITIONAL territory. Council scored STRONG; one notch lower here because V8 measures whether catalyst will FORCE repricing, and AIM-derate-persistence is the wedge that makes the operational catalyst's repricing response uncertain.

fairValue.probWeightedFV: 406p (PV today) — implies +60% upside vs ~254p current. Cross-check: council `all` 2026-04-24 PW-FV 404.9p (within 0.3% — strong independent convergence)
fairValue.thesisType: Primary Type 2 (Turnaround / Margin Inflection — Skinner-Baker post-restructuring torque) + Secondary Type 3 (SOTP — three segments with materially different margin profiles) + Tertiary Type 5 opti...

blindspots: **£150M RCF covenant headroom (Indicator #7 DATA GAP)** — FY25 Annual Report uses "operates well within" language without quantification. Could be 50% headroom or 32%. Resolution: H1 2026 interim disclosure OR FY25 statutory accounts at Companies House (~Jun-Sep 2026). Until quantified, an unexpected weak quarter could trigger covenant pressure that's not visible in the dashboard. Proxy: net debt / adj EBITDA trajectory (Indicator #8).

**Public-sector procurement reform timing** — 40% revenue exposure to HMRC + DWP + HM Land Registry + NHS. UK fiscal pressure could trigger contract re-tendering, framework consolidation, or unit-pricing reductions. No reliable forward signal — only retrospective contract-loss announcements. Watch UK Treasury Spending Review windows + HMT efficiency reviews. **2026-04-27 ADDITIONS:** (a) **Cabinet Office Public Interest Test 25 March 2026** — gov departments must assess in-house delivery for contracts ≥£1m before outsourcing (>95% of central gov spend); described as "biggest wave of insourcing in a generation." Direct policy risk for HMRC inbound mailroom, DWP digital mailroom, Synertec NHS Notify framework, HMLR, NHS Foundation Trust scanning. The argument that records storage is "rarely feasible to insource given specialist infrastructure and Restore's existing custody" is INFERENCE not statutory carve-out. (b) **Two-tier workforce / Employment Rights Act effective October 2026** — "no less favourable treatment" rules for outsourced public-sector workers will raise bid costs for 2nd-generation outsourcing. (c) **SR25 admin-budget cuts 11%/16% real-terms 2028-29/2029-30 + £150m civil-service exit schemes** — ambiguous (could accelerate scanning demand OR compress contract pricing).

**Customer cloud-migration intent (forward-looking box withdrawals)** — Today's 22M stable box count is snapshot, not forward. Customer 2027-2029 digital-maturity tipping points are not yet in withdrawal data. Iron Mountain pre-REIT pattern (2018-2022 contraction before pivot) is the bear analogue. Proxy: NEW box intake rate by industry vertical (financial services vs NHS vs legal) — RST does not disclose.

**Hidden RE realizable value** — Freehold L&B £37.9m NBV vs ~£55-75m estimated market value (web-informed triangulation). Resolution requires either: (a) RICS valuation publication, (b) sale-and-leaseback transaction, (c) strategic-buyer due diligence. None scheduled. Bull-case-only optionality (+13-27p/share).
**Skinner / Baker key-person risk** — 2-year track record (appointed late 2023). Through-cycle resilience untested. Proxy: NED stability + buyback discipline + earn-out completion behavior.

**AIM-derate structural vs cyclical** — DEBATES "Consensus Pricing" left OPEN. If H1 2026 interim delivers IM organic ≥0% / margin ≥21% / Datashred ≥13% AND multiple stays <5.5x, AIM-listing-derate is structural (V8 stays AVG indefinitely). If multiple expands to ≥6x post-delivery, derate was cyclical/lag (V8 → STRONG, MIA Clarity → STRONG, band → CORE 15%).

cyclical.context: Mismatch is the thesis. Daily moves of 2-5% are AIM-noise. Track 30-day MA + relative-to-AIM-100

cyclical.context: <1.20 cable = mild headwind for IRM-anchored multiple comp; >1.30 = mild tailwind

cyclical.context: Adjusting items will compress as Synertec earn-out unwinds (FY26-FY28); statutory will converge upward to adj. Headlines that emphasize statutory weakness are noise unless adjusting items expand.
cyclical.current: FY25 statutory £7.7m vs adj £40.6m; £32.9m adjusting items (legal restructuring + Synertec acquisition costs)
cyclical.name: Statutory vs Adjusted PBT noise

noiseFilter: **Daily AIM share-price moves on thin volume** — RST is AIM-listed with thin sell-side coverage and IHT-related forced-selling overhang. 5-10% daily moves on <£500k volume are noise. Only volume >£2M signals institutional interest. AIM derate persistence is itself the wedge — track over 6+ months, not daily.

**Statutory PBT headlines** — FY25 statutory £7.7m vs adj £40.6m = £32.9m adjusting items (legal restructuring + Synertec acquisition costs). Adjusting items mechanically compress as Synertec earn-out unwinds. Headlines emphasizing statutory weakness are noise unless adjusting items EXPAND beyond £35m or new categories appear.

**Iron Mountain (NYSE: IRM) US-business commentary** — IRM's data-center pivot and US commercial trends are NOT a leading indicator for RST UK B2B. The 22M box count at RST is anchored to UK regulated industries (financial services + legal + NHS records-retention statutes) with different demand dynamics than IRM US.

**AIM-100 / FTSE Small Cap index moves** — Sector-wide AIM weakness on macro headlines (sterling, gilts, retail-investor sentiment) drags RST without thesis implication. Only act when RST drifts >1.5σ from the index over 2+ weeks.

**Sell-side broker PT changes without segment modeling** — Most UK small-cap brokers (Liberum, Numis, Peel Hunt) do not separately model IM box count + Datashred margin path + Technology turnaround + Synertec accretion. PT changes that don't reference these segments are noise. Only react to PT changes that explicitly cite ≥2 of these 4 levers.

**Synertec earn-out absolute magnitude** — £10m earn-out remaining is self-mitigating (RST pays less if Synertec under-delivers). Headlines about earn-out level are not signal — what matters is the underlying NHS share trajectory (25% → 35% target).
